findings.description: The source unit bundles two distinct requirements: (1) attribution formatting/styling for patient quotes (copy_editorial) and (2) a mandatory pairing with warm lifestyle imagery (a layout/assembly concern). Clustering both under a single copy_editorial rule loses the imagery-pairing obligation, which has different governance implications (image sourcing/brand tone) than text styling.

new_rules.scope: patient quote attribution format and styling (name/patient label, italic/distinguished styling)
patient quotes must be paired with warm lifestyle imagery

findings.description: Source unit cites a governance reference (§1.2) tying the footnote-marker requirement to a specific policy/legal section, but the candidate rule carries no citation or governance facet capturing this cross-reference, so traceability to the underlying policy section is lost.

payload.note: add citation/governance facet linking footnote marker requirement to policy §1.2

findings.description: The source unit enumerates six distinct locked elements (Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, ISI). The candidate rule collapses these into a single unscoped 'legal elements' entity without preserving the explicit enumeration, risking under-specification of scope for downstream compliance checks (e.g., a reviewer might miss that job code or Indication is included).

payload.note: explicitly enumerate locked components covered by must_not-reproduce-in-sections constraint
